cards.title: Alcohol
cards.text: Not sold or permitted on local islands, by law. It's available at resorts and on licensed liveaboards, but not on inhabited islands — no exceptions, including bringing your own.

cards.title: Swimwear
cards.text: Bikinis and swim trunks are fine on the island's designated "bikini beach." Elsewhere on the island, modest dress (shoulders and knees covered) is expected of both women and men.

cards.title: Ramadan
cards.text: During Ramadan, visible eating, drinking, or smoking in public during daylight hours is discouraged out of respect — restaurants may adjust hours, and it's worth planning around this if traveling during the holy month.

cards.title: Pets and pork
cards.text: Pork products aren't available on local islands, in line with Islamic dietary law. Dogs are uncommon and not typically kept as pets locally.

cards.title: Friday prayer time
cards.text: Some shops and services pause briefly around midday on Fridays for prayer — a minor scheduling quirk worth knowing, not a disruption to your trip.

cards.title: Public affection
cards.text: Public displays of affection between couples are generally discouraged on local islands, more so than the resort norm.
